script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'wire-argument-planner',
  description: "Wire the argument planner (Sol's +0.0259 lever) into the composer, then an INDEPENDENT adversary tries to break it — the builder does not verify itself",
  phases: [
    { title: 'Wire', detail: 'one agent owns the composer: wire argument_planner + fact_use_ledger + derived outline onto the critical path' },
    { title: 'Attack', detail: 'a SEPARATE adversary attacks the wiring — canary green + boundary held + the planner actually on the path' },
  ],
}

const ROOT = '/home/polaris/wt/flywheel'

const LAW = `
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN) or
OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries NO new particular, MAY be non-entailed — that is
what INSIGHT IS). THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE; the model-written \`claim\` is a display cache
and nothing is validated against it.

\`${ROOT}/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py\` must stay GREEN (currently 25/25). NEVER weaken a check to make
it pass — that is the exact failure that let fabrication ship four times tonight. Run it after every change.

=== TONIGHT'S ONE LESSON ===
THE BUILDER CANNOT VERIFY ITSELF. Four times a module self-tested green while fabrication shipped; the
provenance module passed 18/18 while the P0 ran live on disk; the canary went 16/16 while 6 attacks
succeeded ("it certified a lane the fabrication no longer used"). So this wheel SEPARATES the builder
from the verifier: one agent wires, a DIFFERENT agent attacks.
`

const DEFECT = `
=== THE DEFECT — Sol's #1 lever, +0.0259 in score units ===
${ROOT}/scripts/cellcog_composer.py generates ~28 subsections INDEPENDENTLY in a ThreadPoolExecutor
(around :479-524). NOBODY EVER DECIDES WHAT IS COMPARED WITH WHAT, so the report LISTS findings instead
of ADJUDICATING them. "Critical Synthesis" (w=0.0800, the joint-heaviest criterion in the rubric) scores
6.36 vs the leader's 9.60. bodhi beats us by 0.08 with a SEVENTEENTH of our sources — because it argues
and we enumerate. This is worth more than any corpus change.

=== WHAT EXISTS — WIRE IT, DO NOT REBUILD IT ===
${ROOT}/scripts/argument_planner.py (1,657 lines, 42 functions) is BUILT, TESTED, and imported by NOBODY.
It builds COMPARISON BUNDLES before prose: keys cards by (technology × outcome × industry × unit × method
× horizon × direction), finds the bundles that MATTER (same outcome + different unit = "only look
contradictory"; same unit + opposite direction = genuine conflict; a finding with no counterpart = a
boundary), and assigns each subsection a PLAN (claim-first thesis, ≥2 attributed clauses bound BY card_id,
the exact comparison, methodological comparability, an OWNED verdict, a boundary, a bridge).
${ROOT}/scripts/fact_use_ledger.py is also built + unwired — stops the same finding being narrated 8×.

THE COMPOSER WAS REBUILT TONIGHT: it writes DRAFTS and calls publisher.publish(); the model emits CARD
IDs and NEVER types a journal name; attribution is rendered by report_ast. Do NOT reintroduce surname
inference. Do NOT add a write to outputs/release/ (it is 0555; only publisher writes there).

CARDS: ${ROOT}/outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json — 232 bound, honest, post-quarantine. Do NOT re-mine (paid).
`

phase('Wire')

const wired = await agent(`${LAW}\n${DEFECT}

YOU OWN cellcog_composer.py. You are the ONLY agent editing it. Work carefully and sequentially; run the
canary after every change.

READ FIRST, in order: cellcog_composer.py (the NEW post-rebuild structure), argument_planner.py (its API
and what it returns), report_ast.py (the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED node types the composer emits), research_contract.py
(it has derive_outline that the composer currently IGNORES for a hardcoded OUTLINE at :305).

THEN WIRE:
 1. Before writing subsections, the composer builds COMPARISON BUNDLES from the bound cards and hands each
    subsection its PLAN (thesis, the specific card_ids to compare, the comparison being made, the owned
    verdict slot, the boundary, the bridge). The writer FILLS the plan instead of freelancing 28 isolated
    pieces. Voice stays STRUCTURAL: attributed clauses carry card_ids, owned sentences carry premise_ids.
 2. The dedicated Critical Synthesis section must hold SEVERAL named cross-source syntheses spanning
    industries/methods — not a few residual paragraphs. That is the +0.0259.
 3. Wire fact_use_ledger too IF it fits cleanly; if it fights the new structure, report that and leave it.
 4. If the hardcoded OUTLINE should become research_contract.derive_outline() for generality, do it — but
    keep task 72 working.

DO NOT break the publish boundary. DO NOT weaken the gate. VERIFY BY RUNNING: the planner's bundle-building
is deterministic over the cards, so test it OFFLINE without a big paid compose; run test_gate_is_wired.py
(must stay green). Do NOT commit.

Report: exact call sites you changed, the comparison bundles the planner ACTUALLY found in the real 232
cards (quote them), whether fact_use_ledger went in, canary status, and anything unverified or unfinished.`,
  { label: 'wire the planner', phase: 'Wire' })

phase('Attack')

const attack = await agent(`${LAW}

Another agent just wired the argument planner into cellcog_composer.py. It reported:
${String(wired).slice(0, 2500)}

YOU ARE THE ADVERSARY. You did not write this. Your job is to BREAK it, or prove it holds. Assume it is
broken until you cannot make it fail.

RUN, do not read:
 1. \`python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py\` — must be GREEN, and NOTHING weakened. Check git diff on the
    test file: if any check was deleted or loosened to pass, that is the WORST outcome — say so loudly.
 2. IS THE PLANNER ACTUALLY ON THE CRITICAL PATH, or imported and never called? (This is the exact bug
    that has bitten us all night — provenance.py passed 18/18 while called by nobody.) Prove the composer
    CALLS the planner and that removing the planner CHANGES the output. An AST walk + an ablation.
 3. Does the wiring reintroduce SURNAME INFERENCE or any lane where the model names a source? Grep + test.
 4. Does an owned "verdict" sentence smuggle in a NEW PARTICULAR (a number, a named entity) not in its
    premises? Feed one and confirm it is refused.
 5. Does a comparison bundle ever pair two EXPRESSIONS OF ONE STUDY as if independent (the duplicate-study
    attack)? Test with the Acemoglu NBER-vs-JEP pair if present.
 6. Can the composer still write to outputs/release/ ? It must NOT (0555). Try it.
 7. Do a DRY/OFFLINE bundle build over the real 232 cards and report: how many genuine cross-source
    comparison bundles exist? If the planner finds only 1-2, the +0.0259 is not really available on this
    corpus and you must SAY SO — that is a finding, not a failure.

Report ONLY what you executed. Quote real output. If it is broken, name the file:line and the failing
input. A finding that we are still broken is worth more than a clean report.`,
  { label: 'adversary: planner wiring', phase: 'Attack' })

return { wired: String(wired).slice(0, 1200), attack }

result.wired:
All checks green: compile OK, `--dry` exit 0, gate canary exit 0 (25/25), and the planner and synthesis-contract self-tests still pass (untouched). Nothing committed.

---

## Summary

I wired `argument_planner.py` into `cellcog_composer.py` (the only file I edited: **+238/−9 lines**). The composer now decides *what is compared with what* before any prose, hands each subsection a plan, and appends a deterministic, re-gated cross-source verdict. Gate stays **25/25 GREEN**; planner/AST/synthesis_contract/publisher untouched.

### Exact call sites changed (all in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py`)
- **Import:** added `import argument_planner as AP` (after the `synthesis_contract` import).
- **`WRITE_PROMPT`:** added a `{plan}` slot between `SUBSECTION` and `THE EVIDENCE`.
- **New helper block before `write_report`:** `_OUTCOME_DECOY`, `_BAD_CTX`, `_VERDICT_KIND`, `_outcome_clean`, `_owned_from_planner`, `_verdict_node`, `_boundary_node`, `_plan_brief`, `_assign_comparisons`.
- **`write_report`:** after `admitted` is computed, a planner-build block calls `AP.default_contract()` → `AP.derive_facets` → `AP.find_bundles` → `AP.plan_subsections` → `_assign_comparisons
result.attack:
ADVERSARY VERDICT: The wiring HOLDS on every safety boundary I attacked — I could not make it fabricate or violate THE LAW. But the builder's self-report overstates where the containment comes from, one guard it credits is porous, and the adjudicative VALUE it buys on this corpus is thin and one-note. Details below, all executed, real output quoted.

== THE 7 CHECKS (all RUN) ==

1) GATE GREEN, NOTHING WEAKENED. `python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` → 25/25, `** GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH. **` (ran twice). `git diff HEAD` on test_gate_is_wired.py / synthesis_contract.py / argument_planner.py / report_ast.py / publisher.py is EMPTY — the verifier is byte-identical to HEAD. The ONLY modified tracked file is `scripts/cellcog_composer.py` (+238/−9, matches the builder's claim). Builder ≠ verifier held: no check deleted or loosened.

2) PLANNER IS ON THE CRITICAL PATH (not imported-and-orphaned). AST walk: imported cellcog_composer.py:64, called at :672-678 (`default_contract`→`derive_facets`→`find_bundles`→`plan_subsections`→`_assign_comparisons`). ABLATION: monkeypatched `AP.find_bundles → []` and rebuilt: `planner ON: 219 bundles -> 16 verdict-bundles placed` vs `planner OFF: 0 bundles -> 0 placed`. Removing the planner changes the output. LOAD-BEARING.

3) NO SURNAME-INFERENCE LANE reintroduced. Owned lane refuses a source name (`OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE`, report_ast.py:580); attributed lane refuses a model-typed name — fed `"Acemoglu and Restrepo show…"` → `REFUSED: SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT`. Surname is used ONLY negatively (report_ast.py:313/318 "no lane in which a surname selects a card"). Planner verdict text says "the evidence"/"the estimates", never a name.

4) OWNED VERDICT CANNOT SMUGGLE A NEW PARTICULAR — fed and refused: number → `OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER` (report_ast.py:584 + synthesis_contract.py:178 `\d`); named entity "Microsoft" → `SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:new_entity:Microsoft`. `_anchor_forms` (argument_planner.py:980) only injects VERBATIM span surface-forms, so it cannot introduce an off-span particular.

5) DUPLICATE-STUDY: NO LIVE INSTANCE on this corpus. Acemoglu exists as exactly ONE work/DOI (10.1257/jep.33.2.3); every work_id maps to exactly one DOI (bijective) — the NBER-vs-JEP pair is not present. The planner's own guard `pair_ok` is DOI-level only (argument_planner.py:750-755, `a.doi != b.doi`), which is weak, but the real backstop is work-level: fed two cards from ONE work (Acemoglu JEP) as a synthesis → `REFUSED: premises_share_a_single_source` (synthesis_contract.py:174-175, keyed on rendered attribution, coarser than DOI). Caveat I could NOT exercise: if a future corpus mapped one paper's two DOIs to two DIFFERENT Work nodes, BOTH checks would miss — that is a provenance-graph integrity question, out of scope of this wiring.

6) RELEASE BOUNDARY REAL. `outputs/release` mode `0o555`; `open(release/…, 'w')` → `PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied`. The composer process cannot write the artifact.

7) GENUINE BUNDLES ON THE REAL 232 CARDS (offline): 219 non-refusal bundles raw (193 SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT, 12 SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION, 14 UNCOUNTERED, +306 NOT_A_COMPARISON). But only **16 verdicts actually get placed, across 6 subsections, ALL of one kind: SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT** ("different units, not contradictory / does not establish"). **ZERO genuine cross-source CONFLICTS ship.**

== FINDINGS (ranked; none burns the artifact, but say them plainly) ==

FINDING 1 — The `_OUTCOME_DECOY` guard the builder credits for containment is POROUS, and the real containment is somewhere else. The builder says the decoy (cellcog_composer.py:491-494) neutralizes the Braganza mis-tag. It does not fully: card `manif:fbb547fb6c3c:33573-33842` (Braganza/jbusres) is tagged `outcome=employment` with span *"…creating new jobs… full-time jobs that meet the criteria of decent work… SDG 8…"* — a normative SDG statement, not a finding — and `_OUTCOME_DECOY` MISSES it (it only matches noun+"engagement/satisfaction/…", not "new jobs"). It reaches `_outcome_clean` as clean. On THIS corpus it is harmless only incidentally: that card has an EMPTY `unit_of_analysis`, so the planner independently refuses every comparison on it (0 non-refusal bundles). The robust guard is actually (a) the axis-declared discipline and (b) `_VERDICT_KIND='SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT'` (cellcog_composer.py:510, enforced :582/:622) which structurally excludes all 12 false conflicts by KIND. A mis-tagged employment card that BOTH declared a unit AND slipped the decoy regex would ship a false "different-units" reconciliation. jbusres is absent from all 16 shipped verdicts, so nothing false ships today — but the containment is mislocated relative to the writeup.

FINDING 2 — The builder's own writeup MISIDENTIFIES the false pair (the builder cannot be trusted to describe itself, which is the whole premise). It wrote "Schwabe 2020 (jbusres) × Braganza 2021 (PLOS ONE)". Actual: 10.1016/j.jbusres.2020.08.018 = **Braganza** et al. (J Business Research, 2020); 10.1371/journal.pone.0242929 = **Schwabe & Castellacci** (PLOS ONE, 2021). Surnames and years are swapped. Span-based code is unaffected; the narrative is not reliable.

FINDING 3 — The section titled "Where the literature genuinely disagrees" ships three verdicts that say the findings are **"not contradictory"** (e.g. "These employment findings are not contradictory: … different units of analysis"). Because 0 genuine conflicts exist, the planner backfills the disagreement heading with reconciliations. Within THE LAW (owned voice, no particular, gate-passed: dry run "AST: 54 nodes | 0 unlawful"), but self-undercutting and judge-visible. Some verdict prose is also degraded by verbatim anchor forms — "in studies of medical", "in studies of robot" (`_anchor_forms` lifts bare span tokens).

FINDING 4 — The adjudicative value is real but thin and one-note; the "conflict" value is absent. All 16 placed verdicts are the single move "what holds at the X level does not establish it at the Y level" (24 distinct outcome×unit signatures collapse to 16 placed, dominated by employment/tasks). If the +0.0259 was priced on genuine cross-source disagreement, it is NOT available on this 10-study corpus — every SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION bundle is the one mis-tagged pair, and the design (correctly) never ships it.

Bottom line: safety boundary UNBROKEN across all 7 attacks; ship-blocking defects NONE. Fix upstream (argument_planner.default_contract employment lookahead) so containment stops depending on a missing-unit coincidence, and stop routing "not contradictory" verdicts under a "genuinely disagrees" heading.
